You will know about: what is coaching, its types, components, rules; skills and competencies of coaches, how to proceed a coaching session and coaching methods and tools. After finishing a course you will get: key knowledge about coaching and how to implement it in effective ways


The Crucial Role of Coaching Techniques in Managerial Leadership and Career Development

The role of a manager goes far beyond traditional tasks of overseeing operations and ensuring productivity. A successful manager is now expected to act as a leader, a mentor, and a coach, guiding their team members towards individual and collective success. This paradigm shift highlights the significance of coaching techniques in the realm of managerial responsibilities.


The Transformation of Managerial Roles

Evolving Leadership Expectations

The traditional image of a manager as a stern authority figure has given way to a more holistic view of leadership. Managers are now expected to serve as role models, motivators, and mentors to their teams. This shift stems from the recognition that employees perform at their best when they feel valued, supported, and empowered.

The evolution of leadership expectations can be traced back to the changing dynamics of the modern workplace. In the past, managers were primarily responsible for enforcing rules and ensuring productivity. However, as organizations began to realize that employee engagement and satisfaction were crucial for success, a transformation in managerial roles ensued.

Today, managers are not just tasked with overseeing tasks and projects; they are expected to inspire and empower their teams. This shift is partly a response to the changing nature of work itself. In a knowledge-based economy, where creativity, innovation, and adaptability are paramount, traditional top-down management no longer suffices.


Coaching as a Leadership Skill

Coaching has emerged as a pivotal leadership skill that fosters personal and professional growth. We'll explore how coaching techniques, such as active listening, constructive feedback, and goal-setting, can empower managers to become effective leaders who inspire and guide their teams towards excellence.

Coaching is not a one-size-fits-all approach; it is a dynamic and personalized method of leadership. Effective coaches adapt their style to the needs and preferences of individual team members. They actively listen to understand the unique challenges and aspirations of each employee, fostering a sense of trust and rapport.

Moreover, coaching is not merely about providing solutions but guiding employees to discover answers themselves. This approach instills a sense of ownership and accountability in team members. It encourages them to take the initiative, think critically, and develop problem-solving skills—a valuable asset in today's fast-paced, ever-changing business landscape.

Coaching also plays a crucial role in setting and achieving goals. Effective managers use coaching techniques to collaboratively define clear, actionable objectives with their team members. This process not only aligns individual efforts with organizational goals but also empowers employees to track their progress and make necessary adjustments along the way.


Coaching Knowledge for Career Development

Career Development in the Modern Workplace

Career development is a top priority for employees. Discover how coaching knowledge equips managers with the tools to help team members navigate their career paths, set meaningful goals, and continually improve their skills.

Career development has evolved from being a peripheral concern to a central focus in the modern workplace. In an era marked by rapid technological advancements and industry disruptions, employees recognize the importance of continuous learning and skill enhancement to remain relevant and competitive.

This heightened emphasis on career development places managers at the forefront of helping their team members chart their professional journeys. Coaching knowledge equips managers with the ability to provide guidance that is not only aligned with the organization's needs but also tailored to the aspirations of individual employees.

One of the key advantages of coaching in career development is its ability to facilitate self-awareness. Effective coaches help employees identify their strengths, weaknesses, interests, and values. Armed with this self-knowledge, employees can make informed career decisions that align with their passions and long-term goals.


The Manager as a Career Development Partner

Explore the responsibilities of middle and top managers in preparing and mentoring personal development plans for their team members. Learn how these plans can align with organizational objectives and create a win-win situation for both employees and the company.

Managers who actively engage in career development become indispensable partners in their employees' professional growth. They play a pivotal role in creating personalized development plans that leverage individual strengths and address areas needing improvement.

These development plans not only benefit the employees but also the organization. By aligning individual development goals with the company's strategic objectives, managers ensure that every team member's growth contributes to the overall success of the business.

Furthermore, coaching techniques are instrumental in fostering a growth mindset within teams. Managers can use coaching to encourage employees to embrace challenges, persist in the face of setbacks, and view failures as opportunities for learning and improvement. This mindset shift has a profound impact on team performance and innovation.


Coaching for Internal Education and Employee Development

The Role of Managers in Internal Education

Managers often bear the responsibility of nurturing a culture of learning within their teams and organizations. Explore how coaching methods can be instrumental in identifying and addressing skill gaps, fostering innovation, and supporting ongoing employee development.

Organizations that prioritize learning and development gain a competitive edge. Managers are at the forefront of this initiative, serving as the catalysts for continuous improvement and innovation within their teams.

Coaching methods play a pivotal role in identifying and addressing skill gaps. By regularly assessing the competencies of their team members, managers can tailor training and development programs to bridge these gaps effectively. This proactive approach not only enhances individual skills but also bolsters the organization's overall capabilities.

Moreover, coaching encourages a culture of innovation. Effective coaches inspire creativity and critical thinking in their teams. They challenge employees to question the status quo, explore new ideas, and experiment with novel approaches. In this way, managers act as innovation champions, driving progress and adaptability.


Coaching as a Tool for Employee Empowerment

Employee empowerment is a cornerstone of effective leadership. Coaching techniques provide managers with the tools to empower their team members by fostering a sense of ownership and autonomy in their roles.

One of the primary ways coaching empowers employees is by promoting self-directed learning. Instead of relying solely on formal training programs, employees learn to identify their development needs and take initiative in seeking resources and opportunities for growth. Managers who coach effectively encourage this proactive approach.

Furthermore, coaching cultivates a growth-oriented mindset among employees. When team members receive regular feedback, both positive and constructive, they become more receptive to continuous improvement. They view challenges as opportunities for growth and development, which ultimately leads to higher levels of engagement and performance.

By creating an environment where continuous learning is celebrated, managers inspire their teams to thrive in an ever-changing business landscape. This culture of learning becomes a strategic advantage for organizations, as it enables them to adapt quickly to new challenges and opportunities.


Coaching for Mental Health and Well-being

The Manager as a Trusted Support System

For many employees, especially those who live alone or lack a strong support network, a manager can become their most trusted confidant. Understand how coaching methods extend beyond professional development to support the mental health and well-being of team members.

The relationship between a manager and their team members extends beyond the confines of the workplace. In some cases, a manager may be the primary source of support for an employee, especially when they are facing personal challenges or seeking guidance related to their mental health and well-being.

Coaching methods are not limited to career development; they encompass the whole spectrum of an employee's life. Effective managers create an environment where team members feel comfortable discussing personal challenges and seeking guidance when needed. This trust and openness are essential for maintaining employee well-being.

In situations where employees are dealing with stress, anxiety, or other mental health issues, managers with coaching skills can provide valuable support. They can listen empathetically, offer guidance on stress management techniques, and help employees access appropriate resources for professional help when necessary.


Coaching for Life Questions and Personal Challenges

Life is filled with complexities, and employees often grapple with personal challenges that can affect their work performance and overall well-being. Managers who are skilled in coaching can offer valuable guidance during these times of need.

One area where coaching is particularly beneficial is work-life balance. In today's fast-paced world, employees often struggle to juggle their professional and personal responsibilities. Managers can use coaching techniques to help team members set boundaries, manage their time effectively, and achieve a healthier work-life balance.

Coaching also proves invaluable in navigating interpersonal relationships, both within and outside the workplace. Managers can offer insights into effective communication, conflict resolution, and building positive relationships, which can significantly impact an employee's overall quality of life.

In times of personal crisis or uncertainty, such as major life transitions or unexpected challenges, employees may turn to their managers for guidance. Managers trained in coaching can provide a supportive and non-judgmental space for employees to explore their feelings, identify potential solutions, and develop action plans to address these challenges.


In conclusion, coaching techniques have become indispensable in the modern workplace, particularly within the roles of managers and leaders. These skills not only drive career development and internal education but also play a crucial role in supporting the mental health and well-being of employees.

As we navigate an ever-changing professional landscape, organizations that recognize the importance of coaching will not only foster a culture of continuous improvement but also cultivate a loyal and motivated workforce. Managers who embrace coaching as a core aspect of their leadership style will not only elevate their own careers but also become catalysts for the success of their teams and organizations as a whole.

By understanding and implementing coaching techniques effectively, managers can truly transform the workplace into an environment where individuals thrive, careers flourish, and holistic well-being is nurtured.
targetAudiences: No special requirements. Course for any who want to build the career at management, coaching, mentorship or get to know themselves better, for people working in helping areas, for coaches.

courseDetails.title: Accounting for Fixed Assets and Depreciation.
courseDetails.shortDescription: Managing Fixed Assets and  financial accounting depreciation calculation
courseDetails.longDescription:
Welcome to Accounting for Fixed Assets and Depreciation! This comprehensive course is designed to equip you with the knowledge and practical skills needed to manage fixed assets effectively and understand their impact on financial statements.

The course is structured into five sections, each building upon the last to provide a well-rounded understanding:

  1. Introduction to Fixed Assets:

    • Gain an overview of fixed assets, including the key differences between capitalization and expenses.

    • Explore the lifecycle of fixed assets, from acquisition to disposal, and understand their significance in financial reporting.

  2. Depreciation Methods:

    • Dive into various depreciation methods and learn to calculate them step by step in Excel.

    • Compare these methods and understand their implications for asset valuation and financial statements.

  3. Fixed Assets and Financial Statements:

    • Analyze the impact of fixed assets on key financial statements, including the balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow statement.

  4. Advanced Topics in Fixed Asset Management:

    • Discover advanced adjustments, such as changes in an asset’s useful life, salvage value, and valuation adjustments.

  5. Fixed Asset Planning:

    • Learn how to create fixed asset plans, forecast depreciation, and project fixed asset movements for the next fiscal year.

Case Study:
Apply your knowledge in a practical case study that walks you through the entire accounting process for fixed assets—from acquisition to depreciation and disposal. You'll learn how to record transactions accurately and understand their financial impact.

By the end of this course, you’ll have the confidence to handle fixed asset management and depreciation in real-world scenarios, using practical tools like Excel to ensure accuracy and efficiency.

Enroll now and take your accounting skills to the next level!
targetAudiences: This course is designed for finance professionals, accountants, small business owners, and entrepreneurs looking to enhance their understanding of fixed assets management and depreciation. It is also ideal for students and professionals.

courseDetails.title: Budgeting: Building company budget.
courseDetails.shortDescription: Financial planning:  Budgeting,  forecast and  budget.
courseDetails.longDescription:
This course is designed to equip financial professionals with the essential skills and knowledge needed to excel in budgeting and forecasting. Through step-by-step guidance, practical examples, interactive exercises, and real-world case studies, you will learn to create accurate budgets and forecasts that drive strategic decision-making and business growth.

In this course, you will gain hands-on experience in developing budgets for key financial statements, including the income statement, cash flow statement (both direct and indirect methods), and balance sheet. Additionally, you'll explore best practices for budgeting fixed assets, allowing for more efficient capital allocation and financial planning.

Whether you're new to budgeting or looking to refine your skills, this course will provide the comprehensive knowledge and tools necessary to implement advanced budgeting techniques in a real-world setting. By the end, you'll be equipped to complete a comprehensive budgeting and forecasting project and have the opportunity to earn a professional certification.

Course Highlights:

  • Step-by-Step Learning: Gain practical skills through a structured, step-by-step approach to budgeting, from creating income statements to balance sheets.

  • Comprehensive Focus: Learn how to develop budgets for key financial reports, including the income statement, cash flow statement (direct and indirect methods), and balance sheet.

  • Fixed Asset Budgeting: Master the art of budgeting for fixed assets and capital expenditures, ensuring effective long-term financial planning.

  • Advanced Techniques: Dive deep into advanced budgeting and forecasting strategies, ensuring accuracy and alignment with organizational goals.

  • Real-World Projects: Apply your learning through comprehensive case studies and a final project that consolidates your budgeting and forecasting expertise.

By the end of this course, you will have the tools to confidently develop and manage budgets for all major financial statements, driving business success through effective financial planning.
targetAudiences: Financial Analysts: Professionals who analyze financial data and prepare reports to guide business decisions.
Budget Analysts: Individuals focused on developing, analyzing, and managing budgets within organizations.
CFOs and Financial Managers: Senior executives and managers responsible for overseeing financial operations, planning, and strategy.
Controllers: Professionals tasked with financial reporting, maintaining accounting records, and ensuring compliance with regulations.

Sales and Service Management refers to the strategic planning, coordination, and execution of activities related to the sale of products or services, as well as the delivery of quality customer service. This field encompasses a range of activities, including sales strategy development, customer relationship management, sales forecasting, order fulfillment, and post-sales support.

Importance for Companies:

  1. Revenue Generation: Sales and Service Management directly impact a company's ability to generate revenue. Effective sales strategies and customer service can lead to increased sales and customer loyalty, contributing to the financial success of the organization.

  2. Customer Satisfaction: Providing excellent service is crucial for maintaining customer satisfaction. Satisfied customers are more likely to become repeat customers and recommend the company to others, contributing to long-term success.

  3. Competitive Advantage: A well-managed sales and service function can give a company a competitive edge in the market. It allows organizations to differentiate themselves based on the quality of their products, services, and customer interactions.

  4. Brand Image: Positive customer experiences and effective sales practices contribute to building a positive brand image. A strong brand can attract new customers and retain existing ones, fostering trust and credibility in the market.

  5. Operational Efficiency: Efficient sales and service management processes streamline operations, reducing costs and improving overall efficiency. This can result in higher profit margins and better resource utilization.

Importance for Experts:

  1. Career Growth: Professionals with expertise in Sales and Service Management are often in high demand. Possessing the skills to drive sales and provide excellent service positions individuals for career advancement in various industries.

  2. Versatility: Knowledge of sales and service principles is applicable across diverse sectors. Professionals can transition between industries, applying their expertise to different products and services.

  3. Leadership Opportunities: Understanding how to lead a sales team or manage customer service operations opens up leadership opportunities. Individuals with strong Sales and Service Management skills may advance to managerial or executive roles within an organization.

  4. Entrepreneurship: Entrepreneurs benefit significantly from sales and service management knowledge. Building and sustaining a customer base is crucial for the success of any business, and understanding these principles is essential for entrepreneurs.

  5. Adaptability: The business landscape is dynamic, and professionals with Sales and Service Management knowledge are better equipped to adapt to changes. They can navigate shifts in customer preferences, market trends, and technological advancements more effectively.

Sales and Service Management is integral to a company's success, directly impacting revenue, customer satisfaction, and competitive positioning. For individuals, knowledge in this field opens up diverse career opportunities and enhances their ability to contribute to the growth and success of the organizations they work for or establish.
targetAudiences: No special requirements. Course for any who want to build a career at sales and service management or improve their knowledge.

courseDetails.title: Certified Workplace Harassment Prevention: The POSH Act,2013
courseDetails.shortDescription: POSH Act 2013 in India: Complete Guide to Preventing and Redressing Workplace Sexual Harassment
courseDetails.longDescription:
“This course contains the use of artificial intelligence.”

Workplace safety and dignity are non-negotiable. The Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013 – commonly known as the POSH Act – was introduced in India to ensure every woman can work with equality, respect, and without fear.


This comprehensive course provides a step-by-step understanding of the POSH Act, starting from the Vishaka Guidelines of 1997 to its evolution into a robust legal framework. You will learn the definitions, scope, and objectives of the Act, understand what constitutes sexual harassment, and explore real-world scenarios that help identify unwelcome behavior at work.


The course explains the roles of employers, HR professionals, and government authorities in preventing workplace harassment. It provides detailed guidance on forming Internal Complaints Committees (ICC) and Local Complaints Committees (LCC), complaint filing, inquiry procedures, and the rights of both complainant and respondent.


Learners will also gain insights into compensation, penalties, false complaints, annual reporting, and compliance requirements. To encourage practical implementation, the course covers Indian case studies, international best practices, and strategies to build a culture of zero tolerance.


Whether you are an HR professional, employer, ICC member, or employee, this course will empower you with knowledge, tools, and confidence to create safe, inclusive, and legally compliant workplaces.
targetAudiences: HR leaders, managers, and compliance officers responsible for workplace policies.
Employers, directors, and business owners who must ensure POSH compliance.
Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) and Local Complaints Committee (LCC) members.
Employees and professionals seeking awareness of their workplace rights.
Legal professionals and consultants advising on employment and harassment laws.
Students and individuals interested in gender equality and workplace safety in India.

courseDetails.title: New Labour codes of India | भारत के नए श्रम कोड
courseDetails.shortDescription: New Labour codes of India explained in Hindi
courseDetails.longDescription:
भारत के नए श्रम कानून: एक विस्तृत अध्ययन मार्गदर्शिका


I. पृष्ठभूमि और उद्देश्य


ऐतिहासिक संदर्भ: भारत में ब्रिटिश-युग के श्रम कानूनों का प्रभुत्व कैसे रहा है और स्वतंत्रता के बाद से इनमें बदलाव के प्रयास कैसे किए गए हैं?

वर्तमान सरकार का दृष्टिकोण: श्रम कानूनों में 'संपूर्ण निरसन' की वर्तमान सरकार की प्रतिबद्धता क्या है और इसके पीछे क्या तर्क हैं (जैसे 'ईज ऑफ डूइंग बिजनेस')?

श्रम सशक्तिकरण का महत्व: श्रमिकों के लिए श्रम सशक्तिकरण क्यों महत्वपूर्ण है?

नए कोड का प्राथमिक उद्देश्य: नए श्रम कोड का मुख्य उद्देश्य क्या है? 'आत्मनिर्भर' शब्द के संदर्भ में इसके निहितार्थ क्या हैं?

त्रिपक्षीय प्रणाली: भारत में श्रम कल्याण के लिए नियोक्ता, कर्मचारी और सरकार की त्रिपक्षीय प्रणाली की भूमिका क्या है?


II. श्रम कानूनों का विकास और वर्तमान स्थिति


श्रम कानून का उद्देश्य: किसी भी श्रम कानून का मुख्य उद्देश्य क्या है? क्या यह केवल श्रमिक कल्याण या उद्योग के विकास पर केंद्रित रहा है?

उद्योग समर्थक झुकाव: स्वतंत्रता के बाद के अधिकांश कानून उद्योग के पक्ष में क्यों बनाए गए थे?

ट्रेड यूनियनें: ट्रेड यूनियन अधिनियम, 1926 के तहत ट्रेड यूनियनों के गठन की स्वतंत्रता का क्या महत्व है? उनकी सामूहिक सौदेबाजी शक्ति की भूमिका क्या है?

'हायर एंड फायर' का नारा: नए कोड के संदर्भ में 'हायर एंड फायर' की अवधारणा को कैसे समझा जाता है?

1991 के बाद की मांग: 1991 में अर्थव्यवस्था के खुलने के बाद उद्योग ने श्रम कानूनों में संशोधन की मांग क्यों की?

पुराने कानून: फैक्ट्री अधिनियम, 1948 जैसे कुछ पुराने कानूनों की निरंतर प्रासंगिकता क्या है?


III. संवैधानिक आधार और प्रशासनिक ढांचा


संवैधानिक प्रावधान: भारतीय संविधान के कौन से भाग (भाग III, भाग IV) श्रम कानूनों को जनादेश देते हैं?

मौलिक अधिकार: मौलिक अधिकार (अनुच्छेद 14, 16, 19, 21, 23, 24) श्रम क्षेत्र को कैसे प्रभावित करते हैं?

राज्य नीति के निर्देशक सिद्धांत (DPSP): DPSP (जैसे काम का अधिकार, मानवीय कार्य की स्थिति, मातृत्व राहत, लिविंग वेज) श्रम विधानों को कैसे मार्गदर्शन करते हैं?

श्रम और रोजगार मंत्रालय: केंद्रीय सरकार में श्रम और रोजगार मंत्रालय की भूमिका क्या है?

श्रम समवर्ती सूची में: 'श्रम' को भारतीय संविधान की समवर्ती सूची में रखने का क्या अर्थ है? केंद्र और राज्य सरकारों की भूमिकाएँ क्या हैं?

सूचियाँ (केंद्रीय, समवर्ती): केंद्रीय और समवर्ती सूचियों में श्रम से संबंधित विशिष्ट प्रविष्टियों (जैसे खान, औद्योगिक विवाद, ट्रेड यूनियन, सामाजिक सुरक्षा) की पहचान करें।

कानूनों का प्रवर्तन: श्रम कानूनों के प्रवर्तन में केंद्र और राज्य सरकारों की विभिन्न श्रेणियां और जिम्मेदारियां क्या हैं?


IV. चार नए श्रम कोड का विवरण


पुराने कानूनों का समेकन: कितने कानूनों को चार कोड में समेकित किया गया है? यह एक 'पार्थ ब्रेकिंग' पहल क्यों है?

सामाजिक सुरक्षा का सार्वभौमिकरण: नए कोड संगठित और असंगठित दोनों क्षेत्रों के सभी श्रमिकों को सामाजिक सुरक्षा प्रदान करने के लक्ष्य को कैसे प्राप्त करते हैं?

इंस्पेक्टर राज का अंत: 'इंस्पेक्टर राज' प्रणाली को कैसे समाप्त किया गया है और श्रम निरीक्षकों की भूमिका कैसे बदली है?

असंगठित क्षेत्र पर ध्यान: सरकार असंगठित क्षेत्र (भारत के कार्यबल का 90% हिस्सा) के लिए क्या कर रही है?

प्रधान मंत्री के दर्शन: नए कोड प्रधान मंत्री के दर्शन ("न्यूनतम सरकार, अधिकतम शासन," "आत्मनिर्भर भारत," "सबका साथ सबका विकास और सबका विश्वास") को कैसे दर्शाते हैं?

विवाद समाधान प्रणाली: नए कोड में विवाद समाधान प्रणाली पर क्या ध्यान दिया गया है?


V. विशिष्ट कोड


A. वेतन संहिता, 2019 (Code on Wages, 2019)


समेकित कानून: इसने किन चार पुराने कानूनों को निरस्त किया है?

वेतन की परिभाषा: इस संहिता में वेतन की परिभाषा में क्या बदलाव आया है (शामिल और बहिष्कृत) और इसका क्या प्रभाव है (जैसे पीएफ योगदान)?

न्यूनतम मजदूरी का सार्वभौमीकरण: न्यूनतम मजदूरी को कैसे सार्वभौमिक बनाया गया है और क्षेत्रीय असमानता को कैसे दूर किया जाएगा (राष्ट्रीय फ्लोर वेज)?

न्यूनतम मजदूरी की समीक्षा: न्यूनतम मजदूरी की समीक्षा हर पाँच साल में क्यों की जाती है?

गुजरात मजदूर सभा मामला: गुजरात मजदूर सभा और अन्य बनाम गुजरात राज्य के मामले का क्या महत्व है? इसने श्रमिकों के अधिकारों को कैसे बरकरार रखा और महामारी के दौरान उनके शोषण को कैसे रोका?

संवैधानिक मूल्यों का समर्थन: सुप्रीम कोर्ट ने इस मामले में संवैधानिक मूल्यों (DPSP) को कैसे बरकरार रखा?

कार्यकर्ता और कर्मचारी की परिभाषा: 'कार्यकर्ता' और 'कर्मचारी' की परिभाषा में क्या अंतर है और इसका क्या निहितार्थ है?


B. औद्योगिक संबंध संहिता, 2020 (Industrial Relations Code, 2020)


समेकित कानून: इसने किन तीन कानूनों को निरस्त किया है?

नया कवरेज: पत्रकार और समाचार पत्र कर्मचारी तथा बिक्री संवर्धन कर्मचारी जैसे नए प्रवेशी इसमें कैसे शामिल हैं?

निश्चित अवधि रोजगार: निश्चित अवधि रोजगार को वैध बनाने का क्या महत्व है?

एकल वार्ताकार ट्रेड यूनियन: एक उद्योग के लिए एक एकल वार्ताकार ट्रेड यूनियन का प्रावधान क्या है और यह कैसे मदद करेगा?

शिकायत निवारण समितियां: शिकायत निवारण समितियों की भूमिका क्या है?

स्थायी आदेश: स्थायी आदेश किन उद्योगों के लिए अनिवार्य हैं (कार्यकर्ताओं की संख्या के संदर्भ में)?

हड़ताल और तालाबंदी के लिए नोटिस: हड़ताल और तालाबंदी के लिए अग्रिम सूचना की क्या आवश्यकताएँ हैं?

उपहार और अन्य लाभ: निश्चित अवधि रोजगार के लिए उपहार, ईएसआई, पीएफ, बोनस और अन्य लाभ कैसे प्रदान किए जाते हैं?


C. सामाजिक सुरक्षा संहिता, 2020 (Social Security Code, 2020)


समेकित कानून: इसने किन नौ कानूनों को समेकित किया है?

नई परिभाषाएं: इस संहिता में किन नई परिभाषाओं को पेश किया गया है?

असंगठित, गिग और प्लेटफॉर्म श्रमिक: असंगठित, गिग और प्लेटफॉर्म श्रमिकों के लिए सामाजिक सुरक्षा निधि का क्या महत्व है?


D. व्यावसायिक सुरक्षा, स्वास्थ्य और कार्य स्थिति संहिता, 2020 (Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020)


समेकित कानून: इसने किन 13 कानूनों को निरस्त किया है?

परिभाषाओं का विस्तार और प्रयोज्यता: इस संहिता में परिभाषाओं का विस्तार कैसे किया गया है और इसकी प्रयोज्यता कैसे बढ़ाई गई है?

महिला कर्मचारियों के अधिकार: महिला कर्मचारियों के संबंध में क्या विशेष प्रावधान किए गए हैं?

अंतर-राज्यीय प्रवासी श्रमिक: अंतर-राज्यीय प्रवासी श्रमिकों के लिए क्या विशेष प्रावधान (जैसे यात्रा भत्ता) किए गए हैं?

दैनिक काम के घंटे: दैनिक काम के घंटे की सीमा क्या निर्धारित की गई है?

सलाहकार बोर्ड: राष्ट्रीय व्यावसायिक सुरक्षा और स्वास्थ्य सलाहकार बोर्ड के गठन का क्या महत्व है?

ठेका श्रम पर प्रतिबंध: प्रतिष्ठानों की मुख्य गतिविधियों में ठेका श्रम के नियोजन पर प्रतिबंध का क्या निहितार्थ है?

ऑडियोविजुअल और बीड़ी-सिगार श्रमिक: ऑडियोविजुअल और बीड़ी-सिगार श्रमिकों के लिए क्या विशेष प्रावधान हैं?


VI. प्रभाव और भविष्य


"ईज ऑफ डूइंग बिजनेस" पर प्रभाव: नए कोड भारत में "ईज ऑफ डूइंग बिजनेस" रैंकिंग में सुधार कैसे करेंगे?

दीर्घकालिक प्रभाव: इन कोड का अर्थव्यवस्था और संगठित और असंगठित श्रमिकों के कल्याण पर क्या दीर्घकालिक प्रभाव पड़ने की उम्मीद है?

कोर्स का ध्यान: इस कोर्स का मुख्य ध्यान क्या है (प्रयोज्यता, कार्यान्वयन, प्रभाव, कल्याणकारी उपाय)?

लाभार्थी: ये कोड किन वर्गों के लोगों को लाभान्वित करने वाले हैं (श्रमिक, नियोक्ता, कर्मचारी, प्रबंधक)?


प्रश्नोत्तरी


1. भारत में नए श्रम कोडों को लागू करने के पीछे प्राथमिक प्रेरणा क्या है, विशेष रूप से ब्रिटिश-युग के कानूनों को पूरी तरह से निरस्त करने के संदर्भ में?

भारत में नए श्रम कोडों को लागू करने की प्राथमिक प्रेरणा पुरानी ब्रिटिश-युग की श्रम विधानों को पूरी तरह से निरस्त करना है, जिन्हें अप्रचलित माना जाता था। इसका उद्देश्य श्रम कानूनों के पूरे परिदृश्य को बदलना और भारत में 'ईज ऑफ डूइंग बिजनेस' में सुधार करना है, जिससे आर्थिक विकास और श्रमिक कल्याण को बढ़ावा मिले।


2. नए श्रम कोडों के तहत 'सामाजिक सुरक्षा के सार्वभौमीकरण' से आप क्या समझते हैं? यह संगठित और असंगठित दोनों क्षेत्रों के श्रमिकों को कैसे प्रभावित करेगा?

नए श्रम कोडों के तहत 'सामाजिक सुरक्षा के सार्वभौमीकरण' का अर्थ है कि सरकार का लक्ष्य देश में संगठित और असंगठित दोनों क्षेत्रों के सभी श्रमिकों को पूर्ण सामाजिक सुरक्षा लाभ प्रदान करना है। यह पहली बार है जब असंगठित क्षेत्र के 40 करोड़ से अधिक श्रमिकों को संगठित क्षेत्र के समान लाभों के दायरे में लाया जाएगा।


3. वेतन संहिता, 2019 में 'राष्ट्रीय फ्लोर वेज' की अवधारणा क्यों पेश की गई है? इसका उद्देश्य क्षेत्रीय असमानता को कैसे दूर करना है?

वेतन संहिता, 2019 में 'राष्ट्रीय फ्लोर वेज' की अवधारणा इसलिए पेश की गई है ताकि राज्यों के बीच न्यूनतम मजदूरी में मौजूदा क्षेत्रीय असमानता को दूर किया जा सके। इसका उद्देश्य यह सुनिश्चित करना है कि कोई भी राज्य सरकार इस निर्धारित राष्ट्रीय फ्लोर वेज से कम न्यूनतम मजदूरी तय न कर सके, जिससे पूरे देश में मजदूरी के लिए एक समान आधार स्थापित हो सके।


4. गुजरात मजदूर सभा मामले में सर्वोच्च न्यायालय का निर्णय क्या था? इसने महामारी के दौरान श्रमिकों के अधिकारों और कल्याण को कैसे प्रभावित किया?

गुजरात मजदूर सभा मामले में सर्वोच्च न्यायालय ने फैसला सुनाया कि महामारी के दौरान लॉकडाउन का पूरा बोझ श्रमिकों पर नहीं डाला जाना चाहिए। न्यायालय ने संविधान के निर्देशक सिद्धांतों को बरकरार रखा, जिसमें सामाजिक न्याय, आर्थिक सुरक्षा और कार्यस्थल में गरिमा और समानता को बढ़ावा देने पर जोर दिया गया, जिससे यह सुनिश्चित हो सके कि श्रमिकों का शोषण न हो।


5. औद्योगिक संबंध संहिता, 2020 'निश्चित अवधि रोजगार' को वैध बनाती है। इस प्रावधान का क्या महत्व है और यह श्रमिकों को कौन से लाभ प्रदान करता है?

औद्योगिक संबंध संहिता, 2020 में 'निश्चित अवधि रोजगार' को वैध बनाने का महत्व यह है कि यह इस प्रकार के रोजगार वाले श्रमिकों को स्थायी श्रमिकों के समान सभी लाभ, जैसे कि उपहार, ईएसआई, पीएफ, और बोनस के लिए पात्र बनाता है। यह उद्योगों को लचीलापन प्रदान करता है जबकि यह सुनिश्चित करता है कि श्रमिकों के अधिकारों और कल्याण से समझौता न हो।


6. श्रम कानूनों के प्रशासन और प्रवर्तन के संबंध में भारतीय संविधान की समवर्ती सूची का क्या महत्व है?

भारतीय संविधान की समवर्ती सूची में 'श्रम' का महत्व यह है कि यह केंद्र और राज्य दोनों सरकारों को श्रम से संबंधित मामलों पर कानून बनाने का अधिकार देता है। इसका मतलब है कि श्रम सुधारों को राष्ट्रीय स्तर पर निर्देशित किया जा सकता है, जबकि राज्यों को विशिष्ट स्थानीय आवश्यकताओं के अनुसार कानूनों को अनुकूलित करने की अनुमति मिलती है।


7. 'इंस्पेक्टर राज' प्रणाली को समाप्त करने से क्या तात्पर्य है, और नए श्रम कोडों के तहत श्रम निरीक्षकों की भूमिका कैसे बदली है?

'इंस्पेक्टर राज' प्रणाली को समाप्त करने का अर्थ है श्रम निरीक्षकों की भूमिका को केवल नियामक से सुविधाकर्ता में बदलना। नए श्रम कोडों के तहत, उनकी भूमिका अब न केवल कानूनों के अनुपालन को सुनिश्चित करना है, बल्कि नियोक्ताओं और श्रमिकों को नियमों का पालन करने में सहायता करना भी है, जिससे अधिक सहयोगात्मक और कम दंडात्मक वातावरण बन सके।


8. सामाजिक सुरक्षा संहिता, 2020 असंगठित, गिग और प्लेटफॉर्म श्रमिकों के लिए क्या नया प्रावधान पेश करती है?

सामाजिक सुरक्षा संहिता, 2020 असंगठित, गिग और प्लेटफॉर्म श्रमिकों के लिए एक 'सामाजिक सुरक्षा निधि' का प्रावधान पेश करती है। यह निधि विशेष रूप से इन अत्यधिक असंगठित क्षेत्रों में श्रमिकों को सामाजिक सुरक्षा लाभ और सहायता प्रदान करने के लिए आयोजित की जाती है, जो पहली बार उन्हें व्यापक सुरक्षा जाल के तहत लाती है।


9. व्यावसायिक सुरक्षा, स्वास्थ्य और कार्य स्थिति संहिता, 2020 में अंतर-राज्यीय प्रवासी श्रमिकों के लिए क्या विशिष्ट लाभ शामिल हैं?

व्यावसायिक सुरक्षा, स्वास्थ्य और कार्य स्थिति संहिता, 2020 में अंतर-राज्यीय प्रवासी श्रमिकों के लिए विशेष लाभ शामिल हैं, जैसे पहली बार देश में यात्रा भत्ता का प्रावधान। यह संहिता उनकी कार्य स्थितियों, सुरक्षा और कल्याण को भी सुनिश्चित करती है, जिससे उन्हें विभिन्न प्रतिष्ठानों में नियोजित होने की अनुमति मिलती है।


10. नए श्रम कोड प्रधान मंत्री के "न्यूनतम सरकार, अधिकतम शासन" और "आत्मनिर्भर भारत" के दृष्टिकोण के साथ कैसे संरेखित होते हैं?

नए श्रम कोड प्रधान मंत्री के "न्यूनतम सरकार, अधिकतम शासन" और "आत्मनिर्भर भारत" के दृष्टिकोण के साथ संरेखित होते हैं, क्योंकि वे श्रम कानूनों को सरल बनाते हैं, 'ईज ऑफ डूइंग बिजनेस' को बढ़ाते हैं, और श्रमिकों के कल्याण को सुनिश्चित करते हैं। यह समेकन और सुधार भारत को एक आत्म-निर्भर राष्ट्र बनाने और सभी के लिए समावेशी विकास प्राप्त करने के बड़े लक्ष्य का हिस्सा हैं।


मुख्य शब्दों की शब्दावली

श्रम कोड (Labour Codes): भारत सरकार द्वारा पुराने श्रम कानूनों को समेकित करके बनाए गए चार नए कानून।

निरसन (Repeal): किसी मौजूदा कानून या नियम को औपचारिक रूप से रद्द करना या हटाना।

अधिसूचित (Notified): आधिकारिक तौर पर घोषित या सूचित किया गया, जिससे कोई कानून या प्रावधान कानूनी रूप से लागू हो जाता है।

श्रम सशक्तिकरण (Labour Empowerment): श्रमिकों को उनके अधिकारों, सुरक्षा और कल्याण में सुधार के लिए अधिक शक्ति और नियंत्रण प्रदान करना।

ईज ऑफ डूइंग बिजनेस (Ease of Doing Business): किसी देश में व्यवसाय स्थापित करने और संचालित करने की सुगमता का माप, जिसे अक्सर नियामक बाधाओं को कम करके मापा जाता है।

सामाजिक सुरक्षा (Social Security): स्वास्थ्य देखभाल, पेंशन, बेरोजगारी लाभ, और अन्य कल्याणकारी उपायों के माध्यम से व्यक्तियों और परिवारों को वित्तीय सहायता प्रदान करने वाली सरकारी योजनाएं।

संगठित क्षेत्र (Organized Sector): वे कार्यस्थल जहाँ रोजगार की शर्तें नियमित होती हैं, पंजीकृत होते हैं, और श्रमिकों को भविष्य निधि, ग्रेच्युटी आदि जैसे लाभ प्राप्त होते हैं।

असंगठित क्षेत्र (Unorganized Sector): वे कार्यस्थल जो सरकार के साथ पंजीकृत नहीं होते हैं, जहाँ रोजगार की शर्तें अनियमित होती हैं, और श्रमिकों को अक्सर संगठित क्षेत्र के समान लाभ नहीं मिलते।

ब्रिटिश-निर्मित कानून (British-Made Laws): औपनिवेशिक काल के दौरान भारत में लागू किए गए कानून, जो स्वतंत्रता के बाद भी कई दशकों तक प्रभावी रहे।

श्रम कल्याण (Labour Welfare): श्रमिकों के जीवन स्तर, स्वास्थ्य और काम करने की स्थितियों में सुधार के लिए किए गए उपाय।

त्रिपक्षीय प्रणाली (Tripartite System): वह प्रणाली जहाँ नियोक्ता, कर्मचारी (ट्रेड यूनियन के माध्यम से), और सरकार श्रम संबंधी मुद्दों पर निर्णय लेने के लिए एक साथ काम करते हैं।

ट्रेड यूनियन अधिनियम, 1926 (Trade Unions Act, 1926): एक कानून जिसने भारत में ट्रेड यूनियनों के गठन और उनके कार्यों को कानूनी मान्यता दी।

सामूहिक सौदेबाजी (Collective Bargaining): वह प्रक्रिया जिसमें कर्मचारी, एक ट्रेड यूनियन के माध्यम से, नियोक्ता के साथ मजदूरी, घंटे और अन्य रोजगार की शर्तों पर बातचीत करते हैं।

हायर एंड फायर (Hire and Fire): एक नीति जिसमें नियोक्ताओं को कर्मचारियों को आसानी से नियुक्त करने और बर्खास्त करने की अनुमति होती है, अक्सर न्यूनतम कानूनी बाधाओं के साथ।

आत्मनिर्भर (Atmanirbhar): आत्मनिर्भर या स्वावलंबी। प्रधानमंत्री के "आत्मनिर्भर भारत" के दृष्टिकोण से जुड़ा है।

फैक्ट्री अधिनियम, 1948 (Factories Act, 1948): एक कानून जो कारखानों में श्रमिकों की कार्य स्थितियों, स्वास्थ्य, सुरक्षा और कल्याण को नियंत्रित करता है।

मौलिक अधिकार (Fundamental Rights): भारतीय संविधान के भाग III में निहित मूलभूत मानवाधिकार जो सभी नागरिकों को प्राप्त हैं और राज्य के विरुद्ध लागू किए जा सकते हैं।

राज्य नीति के निर्देशक सिद्धांत (Directive Principles of State Policy - DPSP): भारतीय संविधान के भाग IV में निहित सिद्धांत जो सरकार को कानून और नीतियां बनाते समय मार्गदर्शन करते हैं, जिनका उद्देश्य सामाजिक और आर्थिक न्याय सुनिश्चित करना है।

समवर्ती सूची (Concurrent List): भारतीय संविधान की सातवीं अनुसूची में एक सूची जो उन विषयों को सूचीबद्ध करती है जिन पर केंद्र और राज्य दोनों सरकारें कानून बना सकती हैं।

इंस्पेक्टर राज (Inspector Raj): एक नकारात्मक शब्द जिसका उपयोग उस प्रणाली का वर्णन करने के लिए किया जाता है जहाँ सरकारी निरीक्षक (विशेषकर श्रम निरीक्षक) व्यवसायों को अत्यधिक विनियमित करते हैं, अक्सर भ्रष्टाचार और उत्पीड़न को बढ़ावा देते हैं।

वेतन संहिता, 2019 (Code on Wages, 2019): चार पुराने कानूनों (जैसे भुगतान मजदूरी अधिनियम, न्यूनतम मजदूरी अधिनियम) को समेकित करने वाला पहला नया श्रम कोड।

राष्ट्रीय फ्लोर वेज (National Floor Wage): केंद्रीय सरकार द्वारा निर्धारित न्यूनतम मजदूरी की एक आधार दर जिसके नीचे कोई भी राज्य न्यूनतम मजदूरी तय नहीं कर सकता।

गुजरात मजदूर सभा और अन्य बनाम गुजरात राज्य (Gujarat Mazdoor Sabha and Others vs. State of Gujarat): महामारी के दौरान श्रमिकों के कल्याण से संबंधित एक महत्वपूर्ण कानूनी मामला, जिसमें सर्वोच्च न्यायालय ने श्रमिकों के अधिकारों का समर्थन किया।

औद्योगिक संबंध संहिता, 2020 (Industrial Relations Code, 2020): औद्योगिक विवादों के निपटारे, ट्रेड यूनियनों और स्थायी आदेशों से संबंधित तीन पुराने कानूनों को समेकित करने वाला कोड।

निश्चित अवधि रोजगार (Fixed Term Employment): एक विशिष्ट अवधि के लिए रोजगार का अनुबंध, जो स्थायी रोजगार से भिन्न होता है लेकिन नए कोड के तहत समान लाभ प्रदान करता है।

एकल वार्ताकार ट्रेड यूनियन (Single Negotiating Trade Union): एक उद्योग में केवल एक ट्रेड यूनियन को मान्यता देना जो प्रबंधन के साथ सामूहिक सौदेबाजी कर सके, ताकि विवादों को कम किया जा सके।

शिकायत निवारण समितियां (Grievance Redressal Committees): कार्यस्थल पर कर्मचारियों की शिकायतों को हल करने के लिए गठित समितियां।

स्थायी आदेश (Standing Orders): एक औद्योगिक प्रतिष्ठान में कर्मचारियों की सेवा की शर्तों से संबंधित नियमों का एक सेट।

ग्रेच्युटी (Gratuity): एक नियोक्ता द्वारा कर्मचारी को प्रदान किया जाने वाला एकमुश्त भुगतान, आमतौर पर सेवा की एक निश्चित संख्या पूरी करने पर।

कर्मचारी राज्य बीमा (Employee State Insurance - ESI): कर्मचारियों को स्वास्थ्य देखभाल और संबंधित लाभ प्रदान करने वाली एक सामाजिक सुरक्षा योजना।

कर्मचारी भविष्य निधि (Employees' Provident Fund - EPF): सेवानिवृत्ति के बाद कर्मचारियों को वित्तीय सुरक्षा प्रदान करने वाली एक बचत योजना।

सामाजिक सुरक्षा संहिता, 2020 (Social Security Code, 2020): सामाजिक सुरक्षा से संबंधित नौ मौजूदा कानूनों को समेकित करने वाला कोड।

गिग श्रमिक (Gig Workers): वे व्यक्ति जो अल्पकालिक अनुबंधों या स्वतंत्र कार्य के माध्यम से आय अर्जित करते हैं, अक्सर डिजिटल प्लेटफॉर्म के माध्यम से।

प्लेटफॉर्म श्रमिक (Platform Workers): वे व्यक्ति जो डिजिटल प्लेटफॉर्म के माध्यम से सेवाएं प्रदान करते हैं, जैसे राइड-शेयरिंग या खाद्य वितरण।

व्यावसायिक सुरक्षा, स्वास्थ्य और कार्य स्थिति संहिता, 2020 (Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020): कार्यस्थल में सुरक्षा, स्वास्थ्य और श्रमिकों की कार्य स्थितियों से संबंधित 13 कानूनों को निरस्त करने वाला कोड।

अंतर-राज्यीय प्रवासी श्रमिक (Inter-State Migrant Workers): वे श्रमिक जो रोजगार की तलाश में एक राज्य से दूसरे राज्य में जाते हैं।

ठेका श्रम (Contract Labour): एक ठेकेदार के माध्यम से नियोजित श्रमिक, जो सीधे नियोक्ता द्वारा नियोजित नहीं होते हैं।

courseDetails.title: DCF Valuation: Complete Course with Startup Valuation Case.
courseDetails.shortDescription: Learn how to apply the DCF valuation model to company and startup valuation, with real-world business cases.
courseDetails.longDescription:
This course is designed to equip learners with the skills to perform Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation, specifically tailored for startups and high-growth companies. Unlike traditional DCF models, valuing startups requires addressing unique challenges such as high uncertainty, limited historical data, and rapid growth potential. Through hands-on exercises, real-world case studies, and step-by-step guidance, you’ll learn how to build, analyze, and interpret DCF models for startups.

The course begins with the fundamentals of DCF valuation, covering core concepts like the time value of money, cash flow projections, and discount rates. You’ll then explore how to adapt these principles for startups, incorporating risk adjustments, stage-specific discount rates, and scenario analysis to account for the unique challenges of early-stage businesses. A key focus will be on forecasting cash flows for high-growth companies, where you’ll learn to project revenue, expenses, and free cash flows even with limited historical data.

One of the highlights of the course is a real-world case study where you’ll calculate the DCF valuation for Duolingo, a high-growth startup. You’ll analyze its financials, forecast future cash flows, and determine its intrinsic value, gaining practical experience in applying DCF to a real-life scenario. By the end of the course, you’ll be able to confidently build and interpret DCF models for startups, making informed decisions about valuation, fundraising, and investment opportunities.

This course is ideal for aspiring investors, venture capitalists, startup founders, and finance professionals who want to specialize in startup valuation. Whether you’re looking to value your own business, assess investment opportunities, or deepen your financial analysis skills, this course will provide you with the tools and knowledge to navigate the complex world of startup valuation. With a mix of theory, practical exercises, and real-world applications, you’ll gain the confidence to tackle valuation challenges in the dynamic startup ecosystem.
targetAudiences: DCF valuation

courseDetails.title: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT): From Theory to Practice
courseDetails.shortDescription: A complete guide to the foundations, techniques, and real-world applications of CBT for mental health, personal growth
courseDetails.longDescription:
Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) is one of the most effective, evidence-based approaches in modern psychology used worldwide to treat depression, anxiety, stress, trauma, and many other mental health challenges.


In this course, Mastering Cognitive Behavioral Therapy: From Theory to Practice, you will explore the evolution of CBT from its philosophical roots to its modern applications. You’ll gain a deep understanding of how thoughts, feelings, and behaviors are interconnected, and learn step-by-step techniques to challenge unhelpful patterns and build healthier ones.


Through engaging lectures, clear visuals, and practical exercises, you’ll discover how to:

  • Identify and challenge cognitive distortions and automatic thoughts.

  • Apply powerful tools like thought records, Socratic questioning, and behavioral activation.

  • Use CBT techniques to manage anxiety, depression, and everyday stress.

  • Explore advanced approaches such as Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), and mindfulness-based CBT.

  • Adapt CBT methods for personal growth, coaching, or professional therapy settings.


Whether you’re a psychology student, a future therapist, a coach, or simply someone who wants to better understand the mind—you’ll walk away with the knowledge and tools to apply CBT in real-world situations.


By the end of this course, you’ll not only understand the theory but also be equipped to put CBT principles into practice—empowering yourself and others to think, feel, and live better.


“This course contains the use of artificial intelligence.”

Some parts of this course have been developed with the assistance of AI-powered tools. Specifically, AI was used to:

Generate and refine written lecture content, assignments, and supporting explanations.

Create visual elements such as illustrations and graphics for course material.

Assist in structuring and simplifying complex legal concepts for better learner understanding.

All AI-assisted content has been carefully reviewed, verified, and supplemented by the instructor to ensure accuracy, relevance, and clarity.

This disclosure is made to maintain full transparency and help learners understand the role of AI in the creation of course materials.

Social Media Marketing & Management:


Social Media Marketing (SMM):

Social Media Marketing is a form of digital marketing that utilizes social media platforms to promote products, services, or brands. It involves creating and sharing content on social media networks to engage with a target audience, increase brand awareness, and drive website traffic. SMM encompasses various activities such as posting text and image updates, videos, and other content that sparks user engagement.


Social Media Management:

Social Media Management involves the strategic planning, monitoring, and execution of a company's social media marketing efforts. It includes tasks like creating content calendars, responding to comments and messages, analyzing performance metrics, and adjusting strategies based on data.


Copywriting and Business Writing:


Copywriting:

Copywriting is the art and science of writing persuasive and compelling content with the goal of influencing the reader to take a specific action, such as making a purchase or signing up for a service. Copywriting is prevalent in advertising, marketing, and sales materials, including websites, brochures, email campaigns, and more.


Business Writing:

Business writing is a broader term that includes various types of written communication within a business context. This encompasses emails, reports, proposals, memos, and other documents used for internal and external communication. Effective business writing is clear, concise, and tailored to the audience, conveying information in a professional manner.


Importance for Companies and Managers:


Enhanced Communication:

Social media marketing and management skills enable companies to communicate directly with their audience, fostering brand loyalty and customer satisfaction.

Proficient business writing ensures effective communication within and outside the organization, reducing misunderstandings and promoting a professional image.


Brand Building:

Social media is a powerful tool for brand building. Effective SMM can enhance brand visibility and reputation, leading to increased trust and credibility.

Quality copywriting contributes to a consistent brand voice across various platforms, reinforcing the brand's identity and values.


Customer Engagement:

Social media management allows companies to engage with their audience in real-time, addressing concerns, gathering feedback, and building a community around the brand.

Engaging copywriting is crucial for creating content that resonates with the target audience, increasing the likelihood of interaction and conversion.


Marketing Effectiveness:

Social media platforms provide targeted advertising options. Knowing how to leverage these tools through SMM enhances the effectiveness of marketing campaigns.

Well-crafted copywriting in marketing materials maximizes the impact of promotional efforts, leading to higher conversion rates and ROI.


Career Development and Business Success:


Versatility:

Learning social media marketing, management, copywriting, and business writing equips professionals with versatile skills applicable across various industries and roles.


Competitive Edge:

Professionals who master these skills have a competitive advantage in the job market. Companies value employees who can effectively communicate, market products, and manage social media presence.


Entrepreneurial Success:

For entrepreneurs, these skills are crucial for establishing and promoting their own businesses. Effective communication and marketing strategies can significantly contribute to business success.


Adaptability:

In a rapidly evolving digital landscape, individuals who continuously enhance their skills in these areas demonstrate adaptability and are better positioned for career growth and business development.


The solid understanding of social media marketing, management, copywriting, and business writing is invaluable for companies and managers. It not only facilitates effective communication but also contributes to brand building, marketing success, and overall career development. In the dynamic business environment, these skills are instrumental in staying competitive and achieving long-term success.

Advertising:

Advertising is a form of communication that aims to promote or sell a product, service, or idea. Its purpose is to reach and persuade a target audience through various channels and mediums. The ultimate goal is to create awareness, generate interest, and influence consumer behavior. Advertisements can take many forms, including print ads, radio and TV commercials, online banners, social media posts, billboards, and more. The key elements of advertising include a compelling message, a target audience, and the choice of appropriate media for distribution.


Advertising Management:

Advertising management involves planning, executing, and overseeing an organization's advertising activities. It is a strategic process that requires making decisions about various aspects of advertising campaigns to achieve specific marketing objectives. Key components of advertising management include:


Strategic Planning: Identifying goals, target audiences, and the overall message to be conveyed. This phase involves market research and analysis to understand consumer behavior and market trends.


Media Planning: Selecting the most effective channels and platforms to reach the target audience. This includes choosing between traditional media (TV, radio, print) and digital media (online ads, social media, email marketing).


Creative Development: Crafting the content and visuals of the advertisements. This includes writing compelling copy, designing visuals, and ensuring consistency with the brand's messaging and identity.


Budgeting: Allocating financial resources for advertising campaigns. This involves determining how much money to spend on each campaign and allocating funds to different media channels.


Execution: Implementing the advertising plan, which includes negotiating with media outlets, launching campaigns, and monitoring their progress.


Evaluation and Analysis: Assessing the effectiveness of advertising campaigns through various metrics and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs). This involves analyzing consumer response, sales data, and other relevant factors to measure the campaign's success.


Adjustment and Optimization: Based on the evaluation, advertising management may involve making adjustments to future campaigns. This could include refining the target audience, adjusting messaging, or reallocating budget to more successful channels.


Effective advertising management requires a combination of creativity, market knowledge, and analytical skills. It plays a critical role in helping organizations connect with their target audience, build brand awareness, and achieve marketing objectives. The field is dynamic, with professionals adapting to changes in consumer behavior, technology, and media trends to stay ahead in the competitive landscape.

Public Relations (PR) is a strategic communication process that organizations, individuals, or brands use to build and maintain positive relationships with their target audience, stakeholders, and the public. The goal of PR is to create a favorable image, enhance reputation, and manage perceptions through various communication channels.

PR Management involves planning, executing, and monitoring communication strategies to achieve specific objectives, such as promoting a product, managing a crisis, or building brand awareness. It encompasses activities like media relations, event planning, social media management, and crisis communication.

Learning about PR is crucial for companies and managers for several reasons:

  1. Building and Maintaining Reputation: A positive public image is essential for the success of any organization. PR helps in shaping public perception and building a strong, favorable reputation.

  2. Effective Communication: PR teaches effective communication strategies that allow organizations to convey their messages clearly and persuasively, fostering understanding and positive relationships with the audience.

  3. Crisis Management: In times of crisis, effective PR can help minimize damage to a company's reputation. Understanding how to handle and communicate during crises is a vital skill for managers.

  4. Media Relations: PR equips managers with the skills to interact with the media, ensuring that their messages are accurately portrayed in news outlets and other public platforms.

  5. Brand Building: PR plays a significant role in brand building by creating and maintaining a consistent brand image. It helps in differentiating a company or product in a competitive market.

  6. Stakeholder Engagement: Understanding PR is crucial for engaging with various stakeholders, including customers, employees, investors, and the community. It fosters positive relationships and loyalty.

For managers in different domains, PR knowledge supports career development by:

  1. Enhancing Leadership Skills: PR skills contribute to effective leadership by providing managers with the ability to inspire and motivate teams, especially during challenging times.

  2. Building Relationships: PR fosters relationship-building skills, which are essential in managerial roles for collaborating with internal teams, external partners, and stakeholders.

  3. Strategic Decision-Making: PR knowledge enables managers to make informed and strategic decisions regarding communication, marketing, and overall organizational strategy.

  4. Adaptability: In a dynamic business environment, PR skills help managers adapt to changing circumstances, especially in the face of crises or challenges.

  5. Career Advancement: Managers with PR expertise are often better positioned for career advancement, as they can contribute to the overall success and positive image of the organizations they work for.

In summary, learning about PR is crucial for companies and managers as it helps in building and maintaining a positive public image, fostering effective communication, managing crises, and supporting overall career development in various domains.

courseDetails.title: SQL практикум на базе MySQL (полный курс)
courseDetails.shortDescription: Спроектируем базу данных и разберём все типы запросов с примерами и заданиями.
courseDetails.longDescription:
Этот курс посвящён всестороннему изучению SQL и работе с базами данных на примере MySQL. Материал построен от основ к сложным темам и рассчитан на практическое освоение языка: от установки сервера и настройки окружения до проектирования базы, написания запросов и работы с транзакциями.

Мы начнём с установки MySQL и работы в терминале, познакомимся с графическими IDE (Workbench, DBeaver) и базовыми способами взаимодействия с сервером. Затем разберём структуру базы: первичные и внешние ключи, индексы, типы данных, правила именования объектов, таблицы-справочники и ER-диаграммы. Освоим команды DDL и DML, научимся добавлять, обновлять и удалять данные, фильтровать и сортировать записи, использовать агрегирующие функции, группировку, подзапросы и соединения.

Дальше курс охватывает расширенные возможности SQL: оконные и рекурсивные табличные выражения, представления, хранимые процедуры, пользовательские функции, триггеры, переменные и работу с транзакциями, блокировки и уровни изоляции. Особое внимание уделено анализу запросов (EXPLAIN), оптимизации и предотвращению SQL-инъекций.


Кому будет полезен курс:

  • новичкам, желающим изучить SQL с нуля;

  • студентам для практики проектирования и написания запросов;

  • ИТ-специалистам для систематизации знаний и расширения опыта работы с MySQL.


Чему вы научитесь:

  • проектировать базы данных и работать с таблицами;

  • писать различные SQL-запросы и оптимизировать их выполнение;

  • использовать процедуры, функции, триггеры и переменные;

  • работать с транзакциями, блокировками и анализировать выполнение запросов;

  • безопасно взаимодействовать с базой данных, предотвращая ошибки и SQL-инъекции.


Курс полностью практический, с примерами, заданиями и контрольными вопросами.


Добро пожаловать на курс и успехов в изучении SQL!

Course provided by MTF Institute of Management, Technology and Finance

MTF is the global educational and research institute with HQ at Lisbon, Portugal, focused on business & professional hybrid (on-campus and online) education at areas: Business & Administration, Science & Technology, Banking & Finance.

MTF R&D center focused on research activities at areas: Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science, Big Data, WEB3, Blockchain, Cryptocurrency & Digital Assets, Metaverses, Digital Transformation, Fintech, Electronic Commerce, Internet of Things.

The Chief Technology Officer (CTO) is a key executive in a company responsible for overseeing and strategically managing the technological aspects of the organization. The CTO plays a crucial role in shaping the company's technology vision, driving innovation, and ensuring that technological initiatives align with business goals. The specific responsibilities of a CTO can vary depending on the industry and the company's size, but generally include the following:

  1. Technology Strategy and Vision: The CTO is responsible for developing and implementing the overall technology strategy that aligns with the company's business objectives. This involves staying abreast of industry trends, assessing new technologies, and formulating a long-term vision for the organization's technological landscape.

  2. Innovation Leadership: CTOs are at the forefront of fostering innovation within the company. They lead research and development efforts, explore new technologies, and encourage a culture of creativity and experimentation to keep the organization at the cutting edge of its industry.

  3. Technical Oversight: CTOs provide technical leadership by overseeing the design, development, and implementation of technology solutions. They ensure that systems are secure, scalable, and reliable, meeting the company's current and future needs.

  4. Collaboration with Other Departments: CTOs collaborate with other C-suite executives, particularly the CEO and CFO, to align technology initiatives with overall business strategy. They also work closely with departments such as marketing, sales, and operations to integrate technology solutions that enhance efficiency and effectiveness.

  5. Team Leadership and Development: CTOs build and lead high-performing technology teams. This includes recruiting top talent, fostering a positive and innovative work culture, and providing mentorship to develop the skills of the team members.

  6. Budgeting and Resource Management: CTOs are responsible for managing the technology budget, ensuring optimal allocation of resources, and maximizing the return on technology investments. This involves balancing short-term needs with long-term strategic goals.

  7. Risk Management and Security: CTOs address potential risks related to technology, including cybersecurity threats and data breaches. They implement robust security measures to safeguard the organization's digital assets and sensitive information.

  8. Vendor and Partner Relationships: CTOs manage relationships with technology vendors and strategic partners. They evaluate and select vendors, negotiate contracts, and ensure that external partnerships contribute to the company's technological objectives.

  9. Regulatory Compliance: In industries with specific regulatory requirements, CTOs ensure that the company's technological practices comply with relevant laws and standards. This involves staying informed about changes in regulations and adjusting technology strategies accordingly.

The CTO's role is dynamic and requires a combination of technical expertise, strategic thinking, leadership, and effective communication. As technology continues to evolve rapidly, CTOs play a pivotal role in guiding their organizations through digital transformations and ensuring they remain competitive in their respective markets.


Technology Management and Career Building:

Proficiency in technology management is highly beneficial for career development in various ways:

Strategic Leadership: Technology management equips professionals with the skills to strategically align technology initiatives with organizational goals. This expertise positions individuals for leadership roles, where they can drive innovation, influence decision-making, and contribute to the long-term success of their organizations.

Cross-Disciplinary Collaboration: Technology management often involves collaboration with professionals from diverse fields, such as marketing, finance, operations, and human resources. A solid understanding of technology allows individuals to effectively communicate and collaborate across departments, facilitating career growth and expanding professional networks.

Adaptability and Agility: Technology is constantly evolving, and professionals well-versed in technology management are better prepared to adapt to changes and embrace emerging trends. This adaptability enhances career resilience and opens doors to opportunities in emerging fields and industries.

Problem-Solving and Innovation: Technology managers are often responsible for solving complex problems and driving innovation within their organizations. Building expertise in technology management equips individuals with problem-solving skills, creative thinking abilities, and a mindset geared towards continuous improvement.

Increased Marketability: In today's job market, employers value candidates who possess strong technological acumen. Proficiency in technology management enhances an individual's marketability and expands their career options across industries and sectors.
targetAudiences: No special requirements. Course for any who want to build a career as CIO/CTO or improve their knowledge.

¿DESEAS APRENDER A USAR Y CONFIGURAR LOS PLUGINS DE WORDPRESS PARA SITEGROUND?


SITEGROUND es un servicio de alojamiento web que proporciona alojamiento compartido, alojamiento en la nube, soluciones empresariales, alojamiento de correo electrónico y registro de dominio.  Actualmente proporciona alojamiento para aproximadamente 2,000,000 de dominios en todo el mundo.


SiteGround proporciona servicios de alojamiento web para sitios web de WordPress , Joomla , Magento , Drupal , PrestaShop y WooCommerce.


El servicio de hosting de SiteGround ofrece planes para cualquier tipo de web que tengas en mente.


SiteGround es un hosting multiservicio ampliamente recomendable para cualquier proyecto. Además de hosting, gestiona dominio, webmail, y otros servicios relacionados. Tiene un completo y profesional soporte técnico y atención al cliente dispuesto a atenderte.


¿QUÉ ES SITEGROUND MIGRATOR?


SiteGround Migrator es un plugin gratuito de WordPress desarrollado por SiteGround que nos ayuda a migrar un sitio web con WordPress a tu cuenta en SiteGround desde cualquier otra compañía de alojamiento web.

Para migrar un sitio web en WordPress con el plugin SiteGround Migrator, básicamente hay que realizar los siguientes pasos:

  • Generar un token de migración en el Site Tools de SiteGround.

  • Instalar el plugin SiteGround Migrator en tu WordPress.

  • Pegar el token de migración en el plugin para comenzar la migración.

  • Comprobar tu sitio y actualizar los registros DNS para terminar la migración.


¿QUÉ ES SECURITY OPTIMIZER?


Security Optimizer es un plugin gratuito de WordPress desarrollado por SiteGround para proteger un sitio web de WordPress y prevenir una serie de amenazas como ataques de fuerza bruta, inicios de sesión comprometidos, fugas de datos y más.


El plugin ofrece características como:

  • Evitar el malware común.

  • Defenderte contra los ataques de fuerza bruta.

  • Añadir protección XSS avanzada para proteger carpetas críticas y que no se inyecten archivos maliciosos.

  • Ocultar tu versión de WordPress.

  • Eliminar el archivo readme.txt predeterminado.

  • Proteger el inicio de sesión.

  • Habilitar verificación en dos pasos (con Google Authenticator).

  • Cambiar la URL de inicio de sesión.

  • Impedir usar «admin» como nombre de usuario.

  • Las actividades quedan registradas en el Activity log y además facilita funciones para reaccionar ante posibles ataques desde la sección «Acciones después de un hackeo» para:

    • Cerrar sesión automáticamente a todos los usuarios.

    • Obligar a cambiar las contraseñas.

    • Reinstalar todos tus plugins.


¿QUÉ ES SPEED OPTIMIZER?


Speed Optimizer es un plugin todo en uno para mejorar el rendimiento de WordPress, diseñado y desarrollado por SiteGround. Este plugin tiene todas las funcionalidades que necesitarás para asegurarse de que tu sitio de WordPress funcione de la mejor manera, sin importar en qué plataforma de hosting esté alojado.


Características esenciales del plugin para aumentar la velocidad:

  • Potente caché para tener una web hasta un 20% más rápida.

  • Optimizaciones de portada para minimizar el JS, HTML y CSS, reduciendo el tiempo de espera debido al número de scripts y caracteres de tu código.

  • Optimizaciones de medios para comprimir hasta un 85% el tamaño de las imágenes sin sacrificar la calidad.

  • Optimizaciones del entorno WordPress para optimizar el uso y la eficiencia de los recursos de tu sitio WordPress.

  • Prueba de velocidad y consejos para obtener consejos de optimización a la carta para que tu sitio sea aún más rápido.


Nuestro curso es 100% práctico, lo guiaremos a través de diversos pasos y juntos aprenderemos a usar y configurar los diversos plugins de WordPress para SiteGround, de modo que al finalizar nuestro curso, tendrá más herramientas y funciones extras para su sitio web.

Business English differs from common English in terms of vocabulary, tone, and communication style, as it is specifically tailored for professional settings and interactions within the business world.

Here are some key differences and reasons why learning Business English and Communications is important for companies and professionals:

  1. Professional Vocabulary:

    • Business English incorporates specialized vocabulary related to industries, corporate functions, and specific job roles. This includes terms like ROI (Return on Investment), KPIs (Key Performance Indicators), and other jargon specific to various business sectors.

  2. Formal Tone:

    • Business communication often requires a more formal tone compared to everyday conversations. Learning how to structure emails, reports, and presentations in a professional manner is crucial for effective communication within a corporate environment.

  3. Cultural Sensitivity:

    • Business English teaches cultural nuances that are essential for global communication. Understanding how to navigate cross-cultural communication ensures that professionals can collaborate effectively with colleagues, clients, and partners from diverse backgrounds.

  4. Effective Writing Skills:

    • Business English emphasizes clear and concise writing. Professionals need to be able to convey complex ideas in a straightforward manner, whether it's in emails, reports, or proposals. Clarity in communication helps in avoiding misunderstandings and ensures that the intended message is delivered accurately.

  5. Negotiation and Persuasion:

    • Business English equips individuals with skills in negotiation and persuasion. This is crucial in scenarios such as contract discussions, sales pitches, or team collaborations, where the ability to articulate ideas and convince others is paramount.

  6. Presentation Skills:

    • Professionals often need to present their ideas or reports to colleagues, clients, or superiors. Business English focuses on effective presentation skills, including organizing content, using appropriate visual aids, and engaging the audience.

  7. Networking:

    • Building professional connections is a vital aspect of career growth. Business English provides the tools to engage in networking effectively, whether it's during conferences, meetings, or social events. This skill is crucial for establishing relationships that can contribute to career advancement.

  8. Career Advancement:

    • Learning Business English and Communications can significantly contribute to career advancement. Professionals who can communicate effectively in a business context are more likely to be considered for leadership roles, promotions, and opportunities that require strong interpersonal and communication skills.

  9. Global Business Environment:

    • In today's interconnected world, many businesses operate on a global scale. Proficiency in Business English is essential for professionals working with international clients, partners, or colleagues, enabling smoother collaboration and reducing the risk of miscommunication.

In conclusion, the mastery of Business English and Communications is integral for individuals looking to succeed in the professional arena. Whether one is navigating internal company communication or engaging in global business transactions, effective communication skills contribute significantly to career growth and success in the business world.
targetAudiences: No special requirements. Course for beginners and intermediate users, and any who want to develop Business English skills and increase their level of knowledge.

Digital Transformation:

Digital transformation refers to the integration and utilization of digital technologies in all aspects of an organization, fundamentally changing the way it operates and delivers value to its customers. This involves the adoption of digital tools, processes, and strategies to enhance business operations, improve efficiency, and stay competitive in a rapidly evolving digital landscape. Digital transformation encompasses various elements such as data analytics, cloud computing, artificial intelligence, automation, and the Internet of Things (IoT). It goes beyond mere technology adoption; it's a holistic approach to reshaping business models, customer experiences, and internal processes.


Technology Management:

Technology management involves the planning, implementation, and optimization of technology resources within an organization. It encompasses the strategic use of technology to achieve business goals, enhance organizational performance, and gain a competitive edge. Technology managers are responsible for aligning technology initiatives with overall business objectives, ensuring efficient utilization of resources, managing risks, and fostering innovation.


Importance for Companies and Managers:

  1. Competitive Advantage: Embracing digital transformation and effective technology management can provide a significant competitive advantage. Companies that leverage technology efficiently are better positioned to adapt to market changes, meet customer expectations, and outperform competitors.

  2. Operational Efficiency: Digital transformation streamlines processes, reduces manual efforts, and enhances overall operational efficiency. Technology management ensures that resources are utilized optimally, leading to cost savings and improved productivity.

  3. Innovation and Adaptability: Digital transformation fosters innovation by enabling companies to explore new technologies and business models. Technology management ensures that organizations can adapt to technological advancements, making them more agile and responsive to market dynamics.

  4. Enhanced Customer Experience: Adopting digital technologies often leads to improved customer experiences. From personalized services to seamless transactions, technology plays a crucial role in meeting customer expectations and building brand loyalty.

  5. Data-Driven Decision Making: Both digital transformation and technology management emphasize the importance of leveraging data for informed decision-making. Companies can gain valuable insights from data analytics, enabling them to make strategic choices based on evidence and trends.


Learning and Knowledge for Career Building:

  1. Continuous Learning: In the rapidly evolving tech landscape, continuous learning is essential for managers. Staying updated on the latest technologies and industry trends ensures that managers can make informed decisions and contribute effectively to their organizations.

  2. Problem-Solving Skills: Learning equips managers with problem-solving skills, enabling them to tackle challenges associated with digital transformation and technology management. This ability is highly valued in dynamic business environments.

  3. Leadership and Change Management: Understanding digital transformation and technology management helps managers lead their teams through change. It equips them with the skills to manage resistance, communicate effectively, and drive successful implementation.

  4. Strategic Thinking: Learning about these topics enhances a manager's strategic thinking abilities. It enables them to align technology initiatives with organizational goals and foresee how digital advancements can impact the business landscape.

  5. Networking and Collaboration: Building knowledge in these areas allows managers to connect with other professionals in the field. Networking and collaboration are crucial for sharing insights, best practices, and staying abreast of industry developments.

In summary, digital transformation and technology management are pivotal for companies and managers in today's business landscape. Learning and knowledge acquisition in these domains are instrumental for career growth, ensuring that managers can navigate the complexities of a digital world and contribute effectively to their organizations' success.

courseDetails.longDescription:
¿DESEAS CREAR UN MARKETPLACE MULTIVENDEDOR CON WORPDRESS, WOOCOMMERCE Y DOKAN PARA GENERAR INGRESOS CON MÚLTIPLES TIENDAS DESDE UN MISMO LUGAR?

El Marketplace se refiere a un concepto más amplio de ventas online. En esta plataforma, diferentes tiendas pueden anunciar sus productos, ofreciéndole de esa forma, un abanico de opciones al cliente.

El concepto de Marketplace no es nuevo. En América Latina surgió en 2012 y, hoy en día, muchas grandes empresas digitales ya se han adherido. Algunos ejemplos de Marketplace son Amazon, Google Shopping y Mercado Libre.

Como ya dijimos existen varias diferencias entre un Marketplace y un E-Commerce.

En el caso de un E-Commerce, entras en el sitio web de una tienda X y eliges un producto que es vendido y enviado por la propia tienda.

En el caso de un Marketplace puedes entrar en el sitio web de la tienda X y elegir un producto que está siendo vendido y enviado por la tienda Y.


¿QUÉ ES WORDPRESS?

WordPress es un software gratuito o herramienta de gestión de contenidos, también llamado CMS (Content Management System), diseñada para crear y mantener fácilmente un Blog, un sitio Web o una Tienda Online.


WordPress es sin duda alguna, el mejor gestor de contenidos del mundo, y el más utilizado con diferencia, llegando actualmente a superar el 58% de cuota de mercado. Además es de código abierto y completamente gratuito.

Empezó en 2003 como un software para blogs, pero con el tiempo ha evolucionado como CMS completo con el que podemos hacer sitios web corporativos, tiendas online, sitios de membresía, intranets y mucho más.


¿QUÉ ES WOOCOMMERCE?

WooCommerce es un plugin (complemento) de WordPress que permite implementar una tienda online totalmente funcional en una web.

Este plugin incluye todo lo necesario para empezar a vender en línea:

Las páginas de tienda, carrito, mi cuenta y finalizar compra, los métodos de pago más utilizados en el mundo (transferencia bancaria, PayPal y Stripe), un sistema para configurar diferentes tipos de impuestos y gastos de envío por zonas, etc.

Por supuesto, también permite la subida de un número ilimitado de productos para su venta, pudiendo añadir su precio, descripciones (una corta y una larga), variaciones del mismo, etc.

En definitiva, WooCommerce es una de las mejores opciones que existen para crear una tienda online.


¿QUÉ ES DOKAN?

Dokan es un complemento de múltiples proveedores para WordPress diseñado para ayudar a los proveedores a personalizar las tiendas y administrar el inventario de productos desde una plataforma unificada.

Proporciona una solución completa multivendedor para crear un marketplace con WooCommerce. Su diseño e interfaz súper intuitivos hacen la configuración y gestión de la tienda multivendedor muy sencilla, incluso para principiantes. Su automatización es tan completa que prácticamente sólo tendrás que revisar los pedidos

En nuestro curso utilizaremos las últimas técnicas para crear y diseñar un Marketplace Multi Vendedor, utilizando la plataforma de WordPress y los plugins de WooCommerce y Dokan que nos brindará todas las funciones y opciones necesarias para crear y diseñar nuestro Marketplace Multi Vendedor, que llevarán su negocio a un siguiente nivel.

Nuestro curso es 100% práctico, lo guiaremos a través de diversos pasos y juntos crearemos su Marketplace Multi Vendedor con WordPress, WooCommerce y Dokan, de modo que al finalizar nuestro curso, tendrá su Marketplace Multi Vendedor diseñado con su personalización y a su gusto.

¿QUÉ APRENDERÁS EN NUESTRO CURSO?

  • Cómo crear un Marketplace Multi Vendedor con WordPress, WooCommerce y Dokan.

  • Cómo instalar una plantilla para tu Marketplace.

  • Cómo instalar Dokan en su versión gratuita.

¿POR QUÉ ACCEDER A NUESTRO CURSO?


Nuestro curso de "Crea un MarketPlace Multi Vendedor con WordPress y Dokan", es impartido por el experto y consultor de marketing digital: "Renato Yacolca",  creador de uno de los canales de YouTube más populares del mundo en los temas de tutoriales y marketing digital: Programación Fácil, SEO y Marketing, con más de 185,000 estudiantes (Botón de Plata de YouTube), en el que le mostrará las diversas formas de crear un Marketplace Multi Vendedor con WordPress, WooCommerce y Dokan de forma rápida y sencilla.

Inscríbase ahora y empiece con su aprendizaje en nuestro curso. Hay herramientas interesantes, atractivas y nuevas que aprender con WordPress, WooCommerce y Dokan.

Si tiene alguna pregunta sobre el contenido del curso o algo relacionado con este tema, siempre puede publicar una pregunta en el curso o enviarme un mensaje directo.
